decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The most effective immediate action is to increase the thermal conductivity of the heat spreader to enhance lateral heat dissipation away from the chip center.

changes.reason: Maximizing spreader conductivity within bounds improves lateral heat spreading, directly reducing the localized hot spot under the chip.

expected_effects: More uniform temperature distribution across the spreader layer
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate conductivity or boundary conditions.
Approaching the upper bound of material property; further improvements may require geometry changes.
